title: asparagus verticillatus subsp. floribundus
intro: Asparagus verticillatus subsp. floribundus, also known as the fern-leaved asparagus, is a subspecies of the asparagus plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits associated with this plant include reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of urinary tract infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Asparagus verticillatus subsp. floribundus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for various health purposes.
